title: Đề luyện tập 01 - RISC-V, Cache và Pipeline

questions.title: Câu 1 - Cache set associative, byte addressing
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
2-Way Set Associative Cache
Write policy: Write-back using write allocate (one Dirty bit for a block)
Block size is 64 bytes
Cache size is 128 KB
Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses.

Fill in the following values.

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 128KB / 64B = 2048. Sets = 2048 / 2 = 1024 => index = 10 bits. Offset = log2(64) = 6 bits. Tag = 32 - 10 - 6 = 16 bits. Total = 2048 × (64×8 + 16 + valid 1 + dirty 1) = 1,085,440 bits.

questions.title: Câu 2 - Cache set associative khác cấu hình
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
4-Way Set Associative Cache
Write policy: Write-back using write allocate (one Dirty bit for a block)
Block size is 32 bytes
Cache size is 32 KB
Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses.

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 32KB / 32B = 1024. Sets = 1024 / 4 = 256 => index = 8 bits. Offset = log2(32) = 5 bits. Tag = 32 - 8 - 5 = 19 bits. Total = 1024 × (32×8 + 19 + valid 1 + dirty 1) = 283,648 bits.

questions.title: Câu 3 - Fully associative cache, word addressing
questions.prompt: Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with one-word blocks and a total size of 16 words. Assume one word is 32 bits.

fields.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: One-word block => offset = 0. Fully associative => index = 0. Tag = 8 bits. Cache has 16 blocks. Memory has 2^8 = 256 blocks. Total = 16 × (32 data bits + 8 tag bits + 1 valid bit) = 656 bits.

questions.title: Câu 4 - Fully associative cache với true LRU
questions.prompt:
Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with two-word blocks and a total size of 8 words. Assume one word is 32 bits.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x03, 0x04, 0x10, 0x20, 0x03, 0x24, 0x05, 0x21, 0x30, 0x04, 0x25, 0x31, 0x40, 0x03

Assume a true LRU replacement policy. For each reference, identify the binary word address, the tag, and hit/miss.

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Block size = 2 words => offset = 1 bit. Fully associative => no index. Tag = upper 7 bits = address >> 1. Cache has 4 blocks. Following true LRU, there are 5 replacements and the cache is full after the sequence.

questions.title: Câu 5 - 3-way set associative cache, điền Way theo từng dòng
questions.prompt:
Assume the memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a 3-way set associative cache with 2-word blocks and a total size of 48 words. Assume 1 word = 64 bits.

First, fill in the cache parameters. Then use true LRU. For each reference, identify binary address, tag, set index, offset, hit/miss, and the tags in each way after the reference has been handled.

Lưu ý: Nếu một Way có nhiều dòng như T(index)=tag, T(xxx)=yyyy, mỗi dòng sẽ là một ô nhập riêng.

Sequence:
0x03, 0xb4, 0x2b, 0x02, 0xbe, 0x58, 0xbf, 0x0e, 0x1f, 0xb5, 0xbf, 0xba, 0x2e, 0xce

fields_intro.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Cache blocks = 48/2 = 24. Since this is 3-way, sets = 24/3 = 8, so index = 3 bits. Block offset = log2(2) = 1 bit. Tag = 8 - 3 - 1 = 4 bits. Memory blocks = 2^8 / 2 = 128. Total bits = 24 × (2×64 data + 4 tag + 1 valid) = 3192 bits. Hits occur at 0x02, 0xbf, 0xb5, and the second 0xbf because they access blocks already loaded in the same set. Replacements occur only when set 111 is full: 0x2e replaces T(111)=0000 in Way 1, then 0xce replaces T(111)=0001 in Way 2.

questions.title: Câu 6 - Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: sw
questions.prompt:
Consider a single-cycle RISC-V processor with the simple instruction set: add, sub, and, or, lw, sw, beq.

Let the processor execute the instruction:
sw x12, 120(x5)
at address 2000. Suppose the initial value of register xi is 20i for i = 1,...,31. Suppose the initial value of memory location with address M is M + 1000.

Fill out the control signals and signals at locations A, B, C, ..., R.
questions.solution: For sw, the datapath writes to memory, so MemWrite = 1, RegWrite = 0, MemRead = 0. rs1 = x5 = 100, rs2 = x12 = 240, imm = 120, effective address = 100 + 120 = 220. The branch adder still computes PC + (imm << 1) = 2000 + 240 = 2240, although Branch = 0.

questions.title: Câu 7 - Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: lw
questions.prompt:
Consider the same single-cycle RISC-V processor.

Let the processor execute the instruction:
lw x14, 64(x6)
at address 2004. Suppose the initial value of register xi is 20i for i = 1,...,31. Suppose the initial value of memory location with address M is M + 1000.
questions.solution: For lw, MemRead = 1, MemWrite = 0, RegWrite = 1, MemtoReg = 1. rs1 = x6 = 120, imm = 64, effective address = 184. Data memory returns M + 1000 = 1184, so N = 1184 is written to x14.

questions.title: Câu 8 - Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: add
questions.prompt:
Consider the same single-cycle RISC-V processor.

Let the processor execute the instruction:
add x15, x7, x8
at address 2008. Suppose the initial value of register xi is 20i for i = 1,...,31. Suppose the initial value of memory location with address M is M + 1000.
questions.solution: For add, ALUSrc = 0, MemtoReg = 0, RegWrite = 1, ALUOp = 10, ALU control O = 0010. x7 = 140 and x8 = 160, so the ALU result is 300. Data memory is not read, so M is unknown (?) and the write-back value N is 300.

questions.title: Câu 9 - 5-stage pipeline with forwarding and hazard detection
questions.prompt:
Assume that the following code sequence is executed on the 5-stage pipelined processor with forwarding and hazard detection units shown below:
add x10, x2, x3
lw  x11, 4(x10)
lw  x12, 0(x10)
or  x11, x10, x11
sw  x11, 0(x10)
For the first seven cycles, specify the values of control signals at A, B, C, D, E, F, and G.
questions.solution: This sequence has the same dependency structure as the typical forwarding/hazard exercise: add produces the base register for two loads, then or uses x11 loaded earlier, and sw uses the value from or. Correct rows: 1st/2nd = 1 1 xx xx 1 x x; 3rd = 1 1 00 00 1 x x; 4th = 1 1 10 00 1 1 x; 5th = 1 1 01 00 1 1 1; 6th = 1 1 00 01 1 1 1; 7th = 1 1 00 10 1 1 1.

questions.title: Câu 10 - Execution time: single-cycle vs pipeline
questions.prompt:
Consider the following sequence of 32-bit RISC-V instructions:
add x25, x22, x21
lw  x23, 4(x25)
lw  x22, 0(x25)
or  x23, x25, x23
sw  x23, 0(x25)
a) Assume that it is executed on a single-cycle processor with clock cycle time 1000 ps.
b) Assume that it is executed on a 5-stage pipelined processor with clock cycle time 250 ps and without forwarding or hazard detection units. Insert NOPs to ensure correct execution.
c) Assume that it is executed on a 5-stage pipelined processor with clock cycle time 250 ps and with forwarding/hazard detection units.
d) Which case gives the medium performance?
questions.solution: Single-cycle: 5 instructions × 1000 ps = 5000 ps. Without forwarding/hazard detection, the correct inserted NOPs are: add; nop; nop; lw x23; lw x22; nop; or; nop; nop; sw. There are 5 NOPs, so 10 instructions enter the pipeline and total cycles = 10 + 4 = 14 cycles => 3500 ps. With forwarding/hazard detection, this sequence needs no extra stall because lw x23 -> or has one independent instruction between them, and forwarding handles add -> lw and or -> sw. Total cycles = 5 + 4 = 9 cycles => 2250 ps. Medium performance is case b.
